title: 10 and 20 versions
wikitext:
[]: 1.0 and 2.0 versions             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Thu Aug 22, 2013 7:13 pm Is it completely impossible to watch these first versions of the films? It's a shame that they weren't included in the BDs. I know there aren't many changes, but still, I really want to watch them myself, and wouldn't it be sad if these first versions got lost forever? I'm searching in the internet but I don't find them. Is there any possibility?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Thu Aug 22, 2013 7:49 pm It's impossible to watch the 2.0 one since they never released one and went straight to 2.22. You can probably find the 1.01 one though somewhere on the internet which is pretty much the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Thu Aug 22, 2013 8:26 pm Yes, I know about 1.01, there are almost no changes. But is it really impossible to get 1.0 and 2.0? No camrip, no official release? Lost forever? Thanks for the response.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Aug 22, 2013 8:37 pm Not lost. Discarded.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Thu Aug 22, 2013 8:45 pm Isn't that the same?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Aug 22, 2013 9:03 pm Both 1.0 and 2.0 versions in a vault somewhere. Several places, most likely. Khara releases those versions to theaters and gives copies of it to companies like Funimation for their theatrical releases. After that, everyone's licensed only to release the 1.11 and 2.22 versions on home release. (1.01 was a special case, but even then it's not 1.0.) So, they're not "lost." That would imply no one knows where those copies are. (Like GIANAX truly lost Episode 16 negatives. They can't find them and were most likely accidentally destroyed somehow.) In this case, these companies know where they are, they just aren't allowed or willing to release them on DVD and BD.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Thu Aug 22, 2013 9:28 pm So they aren't lost, but there is no way we can have them.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Thu Aug 22, 2013 9:45 pm kuribo-04 wrote: So they aren't lost, but there is no way we can have them.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Aug 22, 2013 10:04 pm It could be viewed the same way that some people view George Lucas changing up the Star Wars movies. In the case of that saga, I believe that the original, unedited versions are superior to the redone things.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Thu Aug 22, 2013 10:16 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: It could be viewed the same way that some people view George Lucas changing up the Star Wars movies.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Aug 22, 2013 10:18 pm Having the original versions would be cool to have in my collection, but I probably would never watch them. Heck, I never saw 1.01 after 1.11 came out. I really doubt 1.0 or 2.0 would make a difference in my viewing experience.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Thu Aug 22, 2013 10:27 pm I don't think they are superior or inferior (it's not like Hayden Christensen shows up in 2.22 ), it's just that some differences, that aren't pointed out in the Eva Wiki, are interesting. For example, I read a thread some days ago (I don't remember which one it was, I don't even know if it was old or new), and some pages from the 2.0 CRC were posted. The thing is, in the aquarium scene, Asuka's mouth was drawn differently, in a comical way, like in Doraemon. Don't really know how to describe it. Another user (I think it was Kendrix, so if the thread is new, maybe she remembers) pointed out that Asuka's breasts were larger in 2.0 (in the aquarium scene). Somewhere else I've read that Misato had a more comical face in the caffeteria scene with Kaji. All these things seem to indicate that 2.0 had more silliness and fan service, and Anno decided to change that (maybe because the contrast to the second half was too big?). This gives us insight in the making of the Rebuild, and that's why I would like to see these changes myself and look for others. EDIT: I'm still waiting for the day Lucas decides to make a new version of that scene, with nobody shooting and Greedo just dying from a heart attack.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Aug 22, 2013 10:59 pm You have to see the 2.22. 2.0 can't capture Mari's ass as it was meant to be seen, it had to be enhanced.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Thu Aug 22, 2013 11:08 pm Chuckman wrote: You have to see the 2.22. 2.0 can't capture Mari's ass as it was meant to be seen, it had to be enhanced.

[]:             Quote Post by TomasJC » Fri Aug 23, 2013 12:47 am There was a release of 1.0 (however I am unsure if it is the original). It only has english subs, and a few scenes are missing. If I remember it was distributed by Rialto/Red Ant or something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Aug 23, 2013 5:56 am I could survive not seeing 1.0, but 2.0 has the new material, and thus more changes. It seems it is impossible. (OK, I want both.) Thanks for the response. What a strange release. Missing scenes, that can't be official.

[]:             Quote Post by TomasJC » Fri Aug 23, 2013 9:21 pm No problem. However, by missing scenes I mean scenes that were in 1.01 and onward. Not so sure on the exact ones. There is probably a list of changes between the releases on the forum somewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Fri Aug 23, 2013 9:50 pm You can probably find the camrip for 2.0 floating around out there still. I prefer the audio mix in that version. Also, it has the old rape face when Shinji is pulling the skin off Zeruel's core. Much better than 'generic action man'.
